document.title: Literature Study
authorityOrJurisdiction: MFDS Product recall and Suspension of Sale (Korea)
MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices (UK)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer literature study for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by identifying, selecting, appraising, and summarizing literature and recall information relevant to safety and performance.
keyPoints: The device is classified as Class IIb in accordance with Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex VIII Rule 10.
The document states the device has not been marketed or sold yet in the EU.
The literature search used PubMed, European Radiology, and Medical Physics, with a search period of 2000.01 to 2022.12.
The report selected 16 documents from scientific literature databases.
Recall searches were performed for FDA, MFDS, and MHRA sources, and the report states no recalls were related to the products.

evidence.claim: The report concludes there were no recalls related to the products.
evidence.evidenceText: We investigated recalls of similar products in markets such as Korea, the United States, and the United Kingdom, but there were no recalls related to our products.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the device has adequate performance and effectiveness for patients.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, we can prove that our device has adequate performance and effectiveness for patients.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata is ambiguous: source metadata states authoritySignal MFDS/domestic, while the source text primarily references EU MDR classification and also includes FDA, MFDS, and MHRA recall searches.

Appraisal threshold language appears inconsistent: the protocol says to include literature with a score of 14 or higher and exclude literature with a score of 14 or less, while several conclusions classify total score 14 as sufficient.

Literature #10 has conflicting title evidence: the summary table lists 'Effect of digital radiography on emergency department radiographic examinations,' while the later detailed section labels #10 as 'Wireless digital radiography detectors in the emergency area: An efficacious solution.'
Some extracted text contains OCR or encoding artifacts, including '¿ 35 percent' in the FDA recall section and line-break artifacts in article abstracts.
